element_introduction_order.first_appears: Monday onwards - coach ride, town scenarios
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Side-scrolling shooting in varied environments (rides, stampedes, town)

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Monday onwards - jail, bank, saloon sequences

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Boss encounter with named Bad Oil Gang members

element_introduction_order.first_appears: After action stages (conditional on not hitting canteen)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Shooting stars around spinning disk for cash reward
Earned by shooting enemies, lost by wasting bullets/damaging town; used for save progression and determines ending
Regain hit points but disable subsequent bonus stage if hit
Escalating encounters with specific Bad Oil Gang members with different strengths (Snake Oil: fast/poor aim; Tiny Johnson: slow/accurate)

element_introduction_order.element: Moral/mechanical rules (Good Guy Code of the West)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Restricts target selection; creates tactical puzzle scenarios

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Mid-campaign (gang disguises as women; prison escape disguises)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Complicates threat identification and moral code application

typical_relief_triggers: Bonus stages with stars and spinning disk
typical_tension_triggers: Fast draw confrontations with named gang members
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Seven-day structure with repeating daily cycle (shooting practice → action stages → fast draw). Escalating story tension mid-campaign through gang escape, disguises, and false accusations. Relief provided by bonus stages. Multiple action stage types prevent monotony despite arcade-style repetition.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Bonus stages are conditional - disabled if canteen is hit during action stages, creating optional reward/risk decision spaces

difficulty_curve.early_game: Monday-Tuesday: Introduction to shooting practice and basic action stages with initial Bad Oil Gang encounters (coach ambush, saloon shootout, stampede)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Wednesday-Friday: Escalation through train robberies, prison breaks, bank robbery attempts. Introduction of gang disguises (as women) and moral code complications. Tin Star framed and badge revoked by Black Bart. Desert abandonment sequence represents narrative low point.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Saturday-Sunday: Final convergence - rescue Mo from saloon, reveal The Kid is alive, defeat Black Bart in two quick draw encounters, destroy gang headquarters. Ending variation based on final cash score ($0-$750k vs $750k-$999k vs $1M+)
notable_spikes: Mid-campaign false accusation and badge loss
Final showdown with Black Bart (two encounters)

notable_level_patterns.description: Monday through Sunday, each day follows consistent structure: shooting practice warmup → action stages (third-person and/or first-person) → fast draw duel. Provides rhythm while varying specific scenarios.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Monday: coach ride (third-person) + saloon shootout (first-person) + fast draw

notable_level_patterns.description: Third-person stages (rides, stampedes, town streets) alternate with first-person stages (jails, banks, saloons) within action sequences, maintaining visual and mechanical variety.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Third-person cattle stampede followed by first-person jail action

notable_level_patterns.description: Each fast draw concludes a day and features a specific Bad Oil Gang member with unique mechanical characteristics (speed vs accuracy, strength vs weakness).
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Fast draw with Snake Oil (fast gunslinger, poor aim) vs Tiny Johnson (slow, very accurate)

notable_level_patterns.description: Shooting practice canteen creates decision point: hit it for healing but lose bonus stage, or avoid it and gain bonus stage cash reward. Creates risk/reward spatial decision.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Bonus stage: shoot stars orbiting spinning disk woman for cash

notable_level_patterns.description: Mid-campaign introduces 'Good Guy Code of the West' restriction (cannot shoot unarmed women/children), forcing identification and targeting strategy changes.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Gang disguised as women in town shootout - must identify armed vs unarmed or exploit loopholes
